attacking_style: Deploys extreme-range breakaway infiltrations (70km+ solo efforts) targeting GC positions from distance rather than summit finishes. Prefers counter-attacks on second/penultimate climbs but lacks explosive acceleration to sustain sharp GC attacks.
team_tactics: Functions as hybrid GC support/opportunist—UAE/Astana teams use him for late-race isolation attacks on favorites when protected rider status unclear. Teams willing to sacrifice his GC position for workload relief or stage hunting.
terrain_preference: Excels on short technical mountain stages and circuit finishes requiring positioning over pure watts. Strong time trialist (top-5 finishes) but vulnerable on sustained steep gradients and explosive second-category climbs where pure accelerations occur.
vulnerability: Poor wet-weather bike handling (crashed twice in decisive GC moments, 2017/2023). Cannot follow explosive accelerations from top-tier climbers. Energy management failures near finish lines. Teams provide inadequate support depth in critical moments.
key_patterns: Infiltrates breakaways for GC positioning rather than stage wins, rising 7th-2nd overall via opportunism (3 instances)
Crashes in wet/technical conditions at crucial GC moments, losing podium-level positions twice (2017 Vuelta, 2023 TdF)
Attacked/absorbed quickly on penultimate climbs when attempting GC moves, lacks sustained explosive power (3 observations)
Strong time trial capacity (top-5 finishes) elevates him beyond pure mountain domestique role
Specialized in Paris-Nice technical finales with consecutive stage wins, circuit/positioning expertise confirmed
Teams immediately neutralize his attacks when threatening favorites, indicating respected GC nuisance status
form_trajectory: negative: 2023 crash with poor team support suggests declining protected status; last competitive GC signal was 2021 Vuelta extreme-range moves
built_from: 2021 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 11
2021 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 18
